acl_rules_empty_deployed_title: You don't have any firewall rules yet.
acl_rules_empty_deployed_subtitle: Create your first rule by clicking the button below.
acl_rules_empty_pending_title: You don't have any pending rules.
acl_rules_empty_pending_subtitle: They will appear here once you create your first rule.
acl_destinations_empty_deployed_title: You haven't created any destinations yet.
acl_destinations_empty_deployed_subtitle: Create your first destination by clicking the button below.
acl_destinations_empty_pending_title: You don't have any pending items.
acl_destinations_empty_pending_subtitle: They will appear here once you add or modify one.
acl_aliases_empty_deployed_title: You haven't created any aliases yet.
acl_aliases_empty_deployed_subtitle: Click the first alias by clicking button below.
acl_form_section_addresses_description: Define the IP addresses or ranges that form the destination of this ACL rule.
acl_form_addresses_label: IPv4/IPv6 CIDR ranges or addresses (or multiple values separated by commas)
acl_form_section_ports_description: Specify the exact ports that should be accessible.
acl_form_ports_label: Manually defined ports (or multiple values separated by commas)
acl_form_section_protocols_description: By default, all protocols are allowed. You can change this configuration, but at least one protocol must remain selected.
acl_destination_form_subtitle: ACL destinations let administrators create reusable destination definitions that can be used in multiple ACL rules.
acl_destination_active_rules_warning: This destination is linked to one or more active ACL rules. Any changes you make here after the destination is deployed will also affect the rules that depend on it.
acl_destination_updated_pending: Destination added to Pending tab and awaiting deployment.
acl_alias_form_subtitle: ACL aliases let administrators create reusable elements that can be used when defining destinations in multiple ACL rules. You must define at least one element in the alias settings.
acl_alias_updated_pending: Alias added to Pending tab and awaiting deployment.
acl_alias_form_component_required: At least one component is required.
acl_rule_form_subtitle: Define who can access specific locations and which IPs, ports, and protocols are allowed. Use firewall rules to grant or restrict access while keeping your network secure and controlled.
acl_rule_submit_success: Rule added to Pending tab and awaiting deployment.
acl_rule_locations_description: Specify which locations this rule applies to. You can include all available locations or choose specific ones based on your requirements.
acl_rule_location_access_default_allow: Location access allowed by default - network traffic not explicitly defined by the rules will be passed.
acl_rule_location_access_default_deny: Location access denied by default - network traffic not explicitly defined by the rules will be blocked.
acl_rule_destinations_description: You can add predefined destinations to this rule to extend its scope. These destinations are configured separately in the Destinations section.
acl_rule_no_predefined_destinations: You don't have any predefined destinations yet - add them in the Destinations section to create reusable elements for defining destinations across multiple firewall ACL rules.
acl_rule_manual_destination_description: Manually configure destination parameters for this rule.
acl_rule_no_aliases: You don't have any aliases to use yet - create them in the Aliases section to create reusable elements for defining destinations in multiple firewall ACL rules.
acl_rule_aliases_description: Aliases can optionally define some or all manual destination settings. They are combined with the values you specify to form the final destination for firewall rule generation.
acl_rule_permissions_description: Define who should be granted access. Only the entities you list here will be allowed through.
acl_rule_limit_access_description: Choose who or what should be blocked by this rule.
acl_rule_error_allow_deny_conflict: The same entry cannot be both allowed and restricted.
acl_rule_error_permissions_required: Configure at least one allowed user, group, or network device.
acl_rule_error_manual_destination_requir: Manual destination settings are enabled. Provide a value or enable Any.
